info.name: Airtable Enterprise API
info.description:
The Airtable Enterprise API allows enterprise teams to manage their account programmatically outside of the Admin panel. It supports managing users, updating access permissions, managing user groups, workspaces, and bases at scale for enterprise deployments.

Contact Support:
 Name: Airtable
 Email: support@airtable.com

item.name: Airtable Remove a User from Enterprise

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').
(Required) The unique identifier of the user to remove.

request.description: Removes a user from the enterprise account. Optionally, a replacement user can be specified to take over shared resources. The response includes details about affected shared and unshared resources.

response.name: Details of the removal including affected resources.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

item.name: Airtable Grant Admin Access to Users

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

request.description: Grants enterprise admin access to one or more users, allowing them to manage the enterprise account through the Admin panel and API.

response.name: The result of the admin grant operation.

item.name: Airtable Revoke Admin Access from Users

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

request.description: Revokes enterprise admin access from one or more users. The users retain their regular enterprise membership but lose admin privileges.

response.name: The result of the admin revocation.

item.name: Airtable Move User Groups Between Enterprises

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

request.description: Moves one or more user groups from this enterprise account to a target enterprise account.

response.name: Result of the group move operation.
